title: taxanthema ferulaceum
side_effects: Taxanthema ferulaceum can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal issues. Ingesting the plant's leaves or flowers can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while external use can lead to redness, itching, and blistering of the skin. Prolonged exposure to the plant's oils or extracts can also cause respiratory problems, including coughing and wheezing. Improper use of Taxanthema ferulaceum can also interact with certain medications, exacerbating conditions like high blood pressure and kidney disease.
